title: Java StringBuilder Mutation
description: Practice StringBuilder append, insert, reverse, and delete operations.
learningGoals: Trace how append, insert, reverse, and delete mutate the same StringBuilder object.
Contrast StringBuilder mutation with immutable String method results.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the final println print?
completionChecks.explanation: append makes ID-42, insert adds brackets, delete removes them, and reverse mutates the same builder into 24-DI before printing.

choices: It returns a new String and leaves builder as ID-42
It mutates builder and also returns that same builder
It fails because reverse exists only on String
It deletes characters from index 0 to 2
completionChecks.explanation: StringBuilder methods such as reverse mutate the receiver and return the same builder, which is the opposite trap from immutable String methods.

completionChecks.prompt: What does builder contain immediately before reverse() runs?
completionChecks.explanation: The insert and append add brackets, then the two delete calls remove the first and last bracket. The builder is back to ID-42 just before reverse mutates it.
